purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.body:
## Purpose

Use this protocol for broad tasks that are too large to safely track only in live chat.

The agent should create a temporary JSON micro-task file, execute it step by step, update statuses during work, then clean it up or promote only durable facts.

when_to_use.title: When To Use
when_to_use.body:
## When To Use

Use task work tracking when a request:

- touches multiple files, modules, systems, or documents,
- has several phases,
- includes many requirements that are easy to forget,
- requires document migration,
- may be interrupted,
- is expected to take longer than one small focused prompt,
- has unclear risks, open questions, or verification steps.

Do not use it for small, local, reversible edits where the tracking overhead is larger than the task.

default_file.title: Default File
default_file.body:
## Default File

For code projects, use:

```text
docs-tech/cache/current_task.json
```

If the project uses another cache directory, use the configured equivalent.

This file is temporary working memory, not project history.

It should normally be untracked. Commit it only when the project explicitly treats task work files as reviewable artifacts.

minimal_schema.title: Minimal Schema
minimal_schema.body:
## Minimal Schema

```json
task:
  title: TBD
  status: active
  source_request: TBD
  done_criteria: []
micro_tasks:
  - key: inspect_context
    status: pending
    result: TBD
  - key: implement_change
    status: pending
    result: TBD
  - key: verify
    status: pending
    result: TBD
questions: []
risks: []
next_suggestions: []
```

Statuses:

- `pending`
- `active`
- `done`
- `blocked`
- `skipped`

workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.body:
## Workflow

1. Create or refresh the temporary task file before broad implementation.
2. Split the request into small, ordered micro-tasks.
3. Mark one micro-task `active` at a time.
4. Update each micro-task with a short result when completed.
5. Add risks, questions, and verification gaps as they are discovered.
6. Before final response, verify every required micro-task is `done`, `skipped`, or intentionally deferred.
7. Remove the temporary file when it has no future value.
8. Promote only durable facts to the right memory layer.

promotion.title: Promotion
promotion.body:
## Promotion

At the end of the task:

- promote durable technical facts to `context-docs/`,
- promote durable decisions to `DECISIONS.json` in code projects or `DECISIONS.md` in non-code user-facing memory,
- promote remaining future work to `_PLAN.json` or `TODO.json` in code projects, or `_PLAN.md` / `BACKLOG.md` in non-code user-facing memory,
- promote shipped behavior or major fixes to `CHANGELOG.json` in code projects when `changelog.enabled` is not `no`, or to a user-facing Markdown changelog when a non-code project uses one,
- keep only the compact current checkpoint in `STATE.json` for code projects or `STATE.md` for non-code user-facing memory.

Do not copy the full temporary micro-task file into project memory.

structured_document_edits.title: Structured Document Edits
structured_document_edits.body:
## Structured Document Edits

For JSON documents with `index` and `items`, use document item scripts for structural moves:

- `tools/documents/migrate_document_item.ps1` to move or copy an item between documents,
- `tools/documents/move_document_item.ps1` to move an item inside one document,
- `tools/documents/insert_document_item.ps1` to add an item at a controlled position,
- `tools/documents/read_document_item.ps1` and `tools/documents/list_document_keys.ps1` for selective reads before editing.

Manual edits are acceptable when:

- changing the document schema itself,
- fixing the parser or migration tool,
- the script is unavailable or broken,
- doing a very small text correction inside one item and no field-level script exists.

If a script cannot run because a runtime is missing, follow `core/SCRIPT-FALLBACK.json`.
